title: [lichen_l2.c:430-432] peer table full returns -ENOSPC but no mechanism to evict stale peers
description:
Category not checked: Resource exhaustion handling

Bug found: When the peer_table is full (CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_MAX_NEIGHBORS reached), lichen_peer_add() returns -ENOSPC with a LOG_WRN but provides no way to recover:

1. No LRU eviction of stale peers
2. No API to query how many peers are active
3. No API to iterate peers for application-level eviction
4. No aging mechanism to remove inactive peers

In a mesh network, peers may join and leave. With default CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_MAX_NEIGHBORS=16, the table fills quickly and cannot accept new legitimate peers.

The replay_table comment (lines 213-223) discusses a poisoning attack where 'an attacker floods spoofed source addresses to evict legitimate peers' - but currently there's NO eviction at all, which is worse: first 16 peers fill the table permanently.

Impact: P3 - Mesh network eventually becomes unable to accept new peers after 16 are registered.
close_reason: Fixed: Added LRU eviction for peer table. When table is full, the least-recently-seen peer is evicted to make room for the new peer. Added last_seen timestamp field to peer entries, updated on successful frame verification and peer add/update.
